title: Community Gardening - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Food > Community Gardening 15 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ANTFARM ANTFARM LEARNING GARDENS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Community members work and learn together by participating in planting, growing, harvesting, and distributing food. Healthy fresh vegetables are grown with organic and sustainable practices. Harvested vegetables are given to those in need, used in the AntFarm Café and Bakery, and used for various youth and community events and classes. 39140 Proctor Boulevard Sandy, OR 97055 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Saturday 8am-5pm Email (503) 668-7962 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BETHLEHEM HOUSE OF BREAD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shopping style food pantry focusing on culturally relevant food and options for those with food allergies, sensitivities, and other dietary or lifestyle preferences such as vegan or vegetarian. Caring and Sharing Store provides, when available, clothing (infant-adult sizes), cleaning supplies, kitchenware (plates, bowls, utensils), personal hygiene supplies (toilet paper, shampoo, toothbrushes, female hygiene products), bedding, and laundry supplies. Also provides a seasonal community garden with 24 raised garden beds available to the public. Accepts donations. 9055 SW Locust Street Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Food Pantry: Sunday/Thursday 1pm-3pm, Tuesday 4pm-6pm Caring and Sharing Store: Sunday/Thursday 1pm-3pm Community Garden: Season opens Sunday, May 11, 2024: Reservation required Donations: Tuesday-Friday 9am-4pm, Sunday 1pm-3pm Email (503) 639-3181 1 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS RANCH VETERANS AND FAMILY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a community greenhouse and garden. Also offers farming, ranching, and beekeeping lessons. 65920 SW 61st Street Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Must be a veteran Hours: Varies by service Email (541) 706-9062 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CORVALLIS ENVIRONMENTAL CENTER STARKER ARTS GARDEN FOR EDUCATION FOOD FOR FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a program where youth and volunteers grow fruits and vegetables for community members. Produce grown in the garden is donated to local food pantries and social service agencies for distribution. 4485 SW Country Club Drive Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Participants: Low-income families Volunteers: Unrestricted Hours: Hours vary by season Email (541) 753-9211 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CORVALLIS ENVIRONMENTAL CENTER STARKER ARTS GARDEN FOR EDUCATION FAMILY PROGRAMMING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers garden, cooking, and nutrition education classes. 4485 SW Country Club Drive Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies Email (541) 753-9211 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FOOD FOR LANE COUNTY FOOD PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects people to emergency and supplemental food programs, such as food pantries, meal sites, gleaning programs, community gardening opportunities, and nutrition education. FOOD for Lane County is a food bank that distributes food to partner agencies and food pantries. Those partners distribute food to people in their service areas. No food is given out through the administrative office. Eligibility: Varies by program, must meet USDA income guideline for food pantry Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 343-2822 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GLEANING NETWORK OF SOUTHERN OREGON FOOD GLEANING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers produce and collects food donations. Provides a community gardening program where community members can grow produce for themselves. Food is distributed monthly and members will be called when pick-ups are available. On average 40 pounds of food will be provided. 201 Bateman Drive #13 and #14 Central Point, OR 97502 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: No standard hours; food pick-up for members is by appointment only Email (541) 665-1500 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GOOD ROOTS COMMUNITY CHURCH FOOD AND BASIC NEEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a weekly emergency food box including bread and pastries. Clothing closet and personal care items are available. Provides a community garden that is open to the public to pick produce. 1908 SE Courtney Road Milwaukie, OR 97222 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Food pantry: Saturday 10am-noon Community Garden: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Clothing and personal items available Saturdays 8:30am-11:30am Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details GROWING GARDENS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a three-year program to residents with limited resources. Garden installation, plant seeds and starts, compost, tools, and educational workshops are provided. Beginning gardeners are matched with more experienced gardeners known as Garden Advisors, providing one-on-one mentoring throughout the season. Youth Grow provides hands-on education in school gardens to help all children feel accepted and empowered to make conscious choices, become stewards of the environment, share their culture and increase community resiliency. 3114 SE 50th Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: -SNAP, WIC, free/reduced priced school meals participants -Elementary schools where at least 50% of the students are eligible for free/reduced price meals -Families at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) -Public workshops: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 284-8420 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JEFFERSON COMMUNITY FOOD PANTRY AND COMMUNITY GARDEN FOOD PANTRY AND COMMUNITY GARDEN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shopping style pantry where people can receive a 3-5 day food supply. Items that may be available include meats (frozen and canned), vegetables and fruits (fresh, frozen, and canned), fresh bread, eggs, and a variety of dry goods. Offers a seasonal community garden. People may help themselves to produce and are encouraged to participate in gardening. 310 N 2nd Street Jefferson, OR 97352 Eligibility: Pantry: Unrestricted; once a month Community Garden: Unrestricted Hours: Pantry: Wednesday 2pm-6pm Community garden: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week June-October (503) 510-7126 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FOOD BANK EASTSIDE LEARNING GARDEN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a space for beginning and experienced gardeners to work together to grow nutritious fruits and vegetables and learn new gardening skills. Website Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (971) 223-3359 1148 Visit Website More Details PHILOMATH COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shopping-style food pantry once per month. Gleaning program distributes gleaned surplus food weekly. Provides a children's clothing closet and diapers. Also offers a community garden and gardening instruction to community members. Donations and volunteers welcome. 360 S 9th Street Philomath, OR 97370 Eligibility: 300% of the Area Median Income June's Kids Kloset: Ages 0-17 Hours: Food pantry: Tuesday 4:30pm-7:30pm, Thursday 9am-noon, 3rd Saturday of the month 12:30pm-3:30pm Gleaning program distribution: Varies June's Kids Kloset: Tuesday 4:30pm-7:30pm, Thursday 9am-noon, 3rd Saturday of the month 12:30pm-3:30pm Lupe's Community Garden: Open seasonally If Philomath Schools are closed due to weather, the food pantry and June's Kids Kloset will be closed as well. Email (541) 929-2499 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD EUGENE MAIN OFFICE ORGANIC GARDEN PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides access to a community garden. 195 W 12th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Unrestricted, primarily focus is Latino and immigrant population Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-6pm Email (541) 687-6227 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND PARKS AND RECREATION COMMUNITY GARDENS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers garden plots for those who would like land to grow their own produce. Opportunities are available to reserve personal garden plots or to work with a group to grow food. 1120 SW 5th Avenue Suite 858 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Unrestricted. Can reserve a plot if you live outside of Portland city limits, but extra fees may apply. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm. Hours may vary at garden sites. Email (503) 823-1612 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SNOWCAP COMMUNITY CHARITIES FOOD PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides fresh food boxes including meat, vegetables, dairy items, bread, baby food, and formula. Pet food is regularly available in the pantry. Limited monthly Senior Home Deliveries provided. People are welcome to receive food boxes two times per month. Also provides TriMet bus fares. The Community Garden is available to low-income families to raise their own vegetables. Water, greenhouse space, and gardening classes are provided. Gardeners give back by giving 10% of their harvest to the pantry. 17805 SE Stark Street Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Food pantry and community garden: Low-income households Formula: Age 2 and under Monthly Senior Home Deliveries: Homebound seniors age 62 and older Hours: Monday/Wednesday 10am-2pm and 6pm-8pm, Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 10am-2pm Email (503) 674-8785 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TILLAMOOK ADVENTIST COMMUNITY SERVICES HERALD CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shopping-style food pantry and a thrift shop that offers bedding, small household items, personal hygiene supplies, and clothing. Offers community gardening opportunities. Accepts donations for the food pantry and thrift shop. 2710 First Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Food Pantry and Thrift Shop: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-2pm Donation Drop-off: Tuesday/Thursday 8:30am-2pm Email (503) 842-4794 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ANTFARM ANTFARM LEARNING GARDENS BETHLEHEM HOUSE OF BREAD PANTRY CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS RANCH VETERANS AND FAMILY CENTER CORVALLIS ENVIRONMENTAL CENTER FOOD FOR FAMILIES GLEANING NETWORK OF SOUTHERN OREGON FOOD GLEANING GOOD ROOTS COMMUNITY CHURCH FOOD AND BASIC NEEDS GROWING GARDENS GROWING GARDENS JEFFERSON COMMUNITY FOOD PANTRY AND COMMUNITY GARDEN FOOD PANTRY AND COMMUNITY GARDEN PHILOMATH COMMUNITY SERVICES PHILOMATH COMMUNITY SERVICES PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD ORGANIC GARDEN PROGRAM PORTLAND PARKS AND RECREATION COMMUNITY GARDENS SNOWCAP COMMUNITY CHARITIES FOOD PROGRAMS TILLAMOOK ADVENTIST COMMUNITY SERVICES HERALD CENTER
